audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua unconquered-territory and east-Jordan allotment register, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Joshua's old age, remaining land, Philistine and Sidonian territories, promise of future dispossession, nine-and-half-tribe allocation, Reuben/Gad/half-Manasseh boundaries, Balaam death notice, non-dispossessed Geshurites/Maacathites, and Levite inheritance theology.

verses.restored: Joshua was old, advanced in days, and YHWH said to him, You are old, advanced in days, and very much land remains to possess.
This is land remaining: all districts of Philistines and all Geshurites,
from Shihor which is before Egypt as far as border of Ekron northward, reckoned to Canaanite; five rulers of Philistines—the Gazite, Ashdodite, Ashkelonite, Gittite, and Ekronite—and Avvim,
from south, all land of Canaanites and Mearah belonging to Sidonians as far as Aphek, to border of Amorite,
and land of Gebalites, and all Lebanon toward sunrise, from Baal-gad below Mount Hermon as far as entrance of Hamath,

all inhabitants of hill country from Lebanon as far as Misrephoth-maim, all Sidonians. I myself will dispossess them from before children of Israel; only cause it to fall as inheritance to Israel, as I commanded you.
Now divide this land as inheritance to nine tribes and half tribe of Manasseh.
With the other half, Reubenites and Gadites received their inheritance that Moses gave them across Jordan eastward, as Moses servant of YHWH gave them:
from Aroer on edge of wadi Arnon and city in middle of wadi, and all plateau of Medeba as far as Dibon,
and all cities of Sihon king of Amorite who reigned in Heshbon, as far as border of sons of Ammon,
and Gilead, territory of Geshurites and Maacathites, all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan as far as Salecah,
all kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and Edrei; he remained from remnant of Rephaim. Moses struck them and dispossessed them.
But children of Israel did not dispossess Geshurite and Maacathite; Geshur and Maacath dwell in midst of Israel to this day.
Only to tribe of Levi he gave no inheritance; fire-offerings of YHWH Divine of Israel are its inheritance, as he spoke to him.
Moses gave to tribe of sons of Reuben according to their clans.
Their territory was from Aroer on edge of wadi Arnon and city in middle of wadi, and all plateau by Medeba;
Heshbon and all its cities in plateau: Dibon, Bamoth-baal, Beth-baal-meon,
Jahaz, Kedemoth, Mephaath,
Kiriathaim, Sibmah, Zereth-shahar on hill of valley,
Beth-peor, slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth,
all cities of plateau and all kingdom of Sihon king of Amorite who reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses struck with chiefs of Midian—Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba—princes of Sihon dwelling in land.
Also Balaam son of Beor, diviner, children of Israel killed by sword among their slain.
Border of sons of Reuben was Jordan and its border. This was inheritance of sons of Reuben according to their clans, cities and their villages.
Moses gave to tribe of Gad, to sons of Gad according to their clans.
Their territory was Jazer, all cities of Gilead, and half land of sons of Ammon as far as Aroer which is before Rabbah,
and from Heshbon to Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim, and from Mahanaim to border of Debir/Lidebir,
and in valley Beth-haram, Beth-nimrah, Succoth, and Zaphon, remainder of kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon, Jordan and its border as far as edge of Sea of Chinnereth across Jordan eastward.
This is inheritance of sons of Gad according to their clans, cities and their villages.
Moses gave to half tribe of Manasseh, and it was for half tribe of sons of Manasseh according to their clans.
Their territory was from Mahanaim, all Bashan, all kingdom of Og king of Bashan, and all Havvoth-jair in Bashan, sixty cities;
half Gilead, and Ashtaroth and Edrei, cities of kingdom of Og in Bashan, were for sons of Machir son of Manasseh, for half sons of Machir according to their clans.
These are inheritances Moses distributed in plains of Moab across Jordan by Jericho eastward.
But to tribe of Levi Moses gave no inheritance; YHWH Divine of Israel, he is their inheritance, as he spoke to them.

translation_notes: Joshua's old age is acknowledged without contempt; the unfinished work is redistributed rather than making his worth depend on personal completion.
The chapter openly states that much land remains and names living Philistine, Geshurite, Sidonian, Canaanite, Gebalite, and other populations, complicating earlier claims of total conquest.
Philistine terminology and several geographic names may reflect later editorial horizons and require historical-linguistic audit.
Verse 6 promises future dispossession while ordering allotment before actual control, demonstrating that the map is aspirational as well as administrative.
Geshurites and Maacathites remain among Israel despite the dispossession ideal; the text's own social reality is more mixed than its conquest rhetoric.
Balaam is labeled diviner and briefly listed among the slain, requiring comparison with Numbers 22-31.
Levi's lack of territorial inheritance is framed as dependence upon YHWH and sanctuary offerings, which creates communal support obligations rather than clerical superiority.
The dense place list is ancient administrative geography, not a modern border instrument or permission to remove current inhabitants.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 13 to justify modern territorial claims, displacement, ethnic mapping, contempt for aging leaders, or clerical privilege. Center honest acknowledgment of unfinished work, shared succession, the continued presence of diverse communities, and maps as historically situated texts rather than timeless deeds.
